steps.title: Spot the bridging requirement
steps.content: The claim identifies a specific technique: using figurative language to connect Washington, DC, and the rural South. The answer must demonstrate this—combine figurative language with an explicit reference to both places.

steps.title: Check which choice bridges both places
steps.content: Choice C uses the figurative phrase “falls like a fertile shower” to depict Southern soil on DC's streets, merging the rural past and urban present through metaphor. Choices A and B use figurative language but confine it to a single location without bridging both places, while D describes DC without figurative language or reference to the South. That matches choice C — C is correct.
